### Post by yoyu007 on 2008-09-03
after i upgrade,my linux kernel update to 2.6.27-2
and my nvidia driver update to 177.10


but the new kernel broked with new nvidia driver

here is my xorg.conf:

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using
# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

[COLOR="DarkRed"]Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection[/COLOR]

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "BenQ FP71G"
    HorizSync       31.0 - 83.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 76.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 6600 LE"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "CRT-0"
    Option         "metamodes" "1152x864 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection



this xorg.conf worked fine for nvidia 177.70 or 173.14 and linux kernel 2.6.26-5,but i dont try the kernel 2.6.27-1

and i tried to blask  line :
 #Section "Files"
  #   RgbPath         "/usr/lib/X11/rgb"
 #EndSection

but it dont work too

anyone can help me,i want to play Nexuiz

### Post by Slug71 on 2008-09-03
> **yoyu007 said:**
> and the shutdown button dont work too,i have to logout ,them shutdown

or i can use command :sudo halt  to shut computer

Everyones having this problem.

And use the nVidia 173 driver. People seem to be having less issues with that one or try rebooting your computor a few times since that seems to have done the trick for a few users.

---

### Post by nanog on 2008-09-03
How to fix nvidia (at least until tseliot fixes dkms bug)

```

cd /var/lib/dkms/nvidia
sudo rm -rf 177* 173*
sudo apt-get install --reinstall linux-image-2.6.27-2-generic

```
You may need to modify "2.6.27-2" with whatever kernel version you are using (e.g. 2.6.27-1 etc.).
Credits: tseliot via plun.

Note: sudo rm -rf is dangerous so be careful with this command.

---

### Post by omgbots on 2008-09-03
I simply had to build the nvidia module for the new kernel.  I'm not sure why it didn't do it automatically.  But I wouldn't remove the old modules just in case you want to boot your older kernel.

Try:

```
ls /var/lib/dkms/nvidia/
```

If there isn't a link for kernel-2.6.27-2-generic-i686, then:

```
sudo dkms build -m nvidia -v 177.70
sudo dkms install -m nvidia -v 177.70
```

then reboot

---

### Post by nanog on 2008-09-03
For some reason the module gets built properly for me only when I remove the module directory and reinstall a 2.6.27 kernel. Others have noted that dkms build/autobuild is sometimes successful after multiple attempts. Also, the dkms auto-build/build feature works fine for me in 2.6.26-5 (so removing the module is probably not a problem for that kernel).

---

### Post by Partyboi2 on 2008-09-03
> **yoyu007 said:**
> and the shutdown button dont work too,i have to logout ,them shutdown

or i can use command :sudo halt  to shut computer
This should be fixed now in the consolekit upgrade

> This bug was fixed in the package consolekit - 0.2.10-1ubuntu4
 ---------------
consolekit (0.2.10-1ubuntu4) intrepid; urgency=low
   * Enable PolicyKit controlled shutdown/reboot functionality (LP: #250506):
    - Disable 01-dbus_policy.patch.
    - Add libpolkit-dev build dependency.
    - debian/consolekit.install: Install .policy file.

### Post by Nullack on 2008-09-03
That xorg.conf is a nightmare. Minimal xorg.conf is key.

---

### Post by jaymode on 2008-09-03
I followed the kernel reinstall post and then used the Restricted Driver Manager to reinstall 177.70 and all works now for me.

---

### Post by kseise on 2008-09-04
> **yoyu007 said:**
> and the shutdown button dont work too,i have to logout ,them shutdown

or i can use command :sudo halt  to shut computer

I found that the default button is not Shutdown, but Logout.  Remove the button from your panel and add the shutdown button to the panel instead.  Otherwise, you can still logout under the System>Shutdown menu option.  See if that helps.  

PS- Ubuntu intrepid (development branch), kernel 2.6.27-2-generic causes me to boot to a black screen and makes it impossible to use my add-on nvidia video card.  I can only use the onboard graphics chip.  I have not been able to get to a command prompt to rebuild the module yet, but I see there is hope.  Thanks guys.  It's getting there.

---

### Post by yoyu007 on 2008-09-04
cd /var/lib/dkms/nvidia
sudo rm -rf 177* 173*
sudo apt-get install --reinstall linux-image-2.6.27-2-generic
sudo dkms install -m nvidia -v 177.70


it works,thanks all of you

### Post by philinux on 2008-09-04
Fixed.

  [B]I copied my xorg.conf from Hardy. 
[/B]
Which has not much in it but it worked.

```
Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver        "kbd"
    Option        "XkbRules"    "xorg"
    Option        "XkbModel"    "pc105"
    Option        "XkbLayout"    "gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
    Driver        "mouse"
    Option        "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Configured Video Device"
    Driver        "nvidia"
    Option        "NoLogo"    "True"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Default Screen"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    Device        "Configured Video Device"
    Defaultdepth    24
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
EndSection
Section "Module"
    Load        "glx"
EndSection

```The xorg that that system created was borked. 

Tried xfix, reconfigure and nvidia-xconfig.

---

### Post by knix on 2008-09-04
I have 2.27-2 installed, with all the updates in synaptic. My nvidia drivers are working. I purged all the nvidia-glx and nvidia-kernel-source packages. I the removed the dkms folders for 177 (I had 177 only). Next, I reinstalled the kernel, then installed 173. When I had both the packages and the .run script versions installed, or didn't delete the dkms folders, I got the dkms multiple versions message.
So I would remove packages and files, then reinstall kernel, then install packages again.
All my glx stuff works except compiz; I'm getting 2400 fps in glxgears on a geforce 84000gs.

Philinux, I tried yours, and it works about the same.

I know its a mess, but here's my xorg.conf

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder63)  Wed Aug 27 13:20:19 PDT 2008


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "yes"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Unknown"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 110.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 150.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"

    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "XAANoOffScreenPixmaps" "true"
    Option         "NoLogo" "False"
    Option         "RenderAccel" "True"
    Option         "AllowGLXWithComposite" "True"
    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by Nullack on 2008-09-04
> **philinux said:**
> Fixed.

  [B]I copied my xorg.conf from Hardy. 
[/B]
Which has not much in it but it worked.

```
Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver        "kbd"
    Option        "XkbRules"    "xorg"
    Option        "XkbModel"    "pc105"
    Option        "XkbLayout"    "gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
    Driver        "mouse"
    Option        "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Configured Video Device"
    Driver        "nvidia"
    Option        "NoLogo"    "True"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Default Screen"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    Device        "Configured Video Device"
    Defaultdepth    24
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
EndSection
Section "Module"
    Load        "glx"
EndSection

```The xorg that that system created was borked. 

Tried xfix, reconfigure and nvidia-xconfig.

So now you fixed it for yourself, wheres the bug :)

---

### Post by philinux on 2008-09-04
DKMS I guess. I saw a fleeting error when the kernel installed regarding nvidia. Something about going to manual.

---

### Post by Nullack on 2008-09-04
> **philinux said:**
> DKMS I guess. I saw a fleeting error when the kernel installed regarding nvidia. Something about going to manual.

tseliot is working on fixing that.

Since youve fixed your other issue with xorg.conf why dont you help the developers by giving them so low hanging fruit with a bug report on what your symptoms were and what you changed in the conf to fix it.

---

### Post by philinux on 2008-09-04
All i did was follow the advice in this thread and use my Hardy xorg.conf

---

### Post by Nullack on 2008-09-04
Why are you so reluctant to try to help out the distro?

Why not compare your hardy conf to your xorg conf and report the differences and how it fixed your issue in a bug? Why do you want someone else to have to go through what you did?

---

### Post by philinux on 2008-09-05
I went to bed. I is on London time! Here's on generated by nvidia-xconfig

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder62)  Wed Aug 27 13:11:49 PDT 2008

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using
# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen      0  "Default Screen" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "v4l"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "gb"
    Option         "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Configured Monitor"
    VendorName     "Plug 'n' Play"
    ModelName      "Plug 'n' Play"
    Gamma           1
    ModeLine       "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -hsync -vsync
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Configured Video Device"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    BoardName      "vesa"
    Screen          0
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "Configured Video Device"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Virtual     640 480
        Depth       24
        Modes      "640x480@60"
    EndSubSection
EndSection
```Here's the one by xfix```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Configured Video Device"
    Driver        "nvidia"
    Option        "NoLogo"    "True"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Default Screen"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    Device        "Configured Video Device"
    Defaultdepth    24
EndSection
Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
    Inputdevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    Inputdevice    "Configured Mouse"
    
    #Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
    #InputDevice     "stylus"        "SendCoreEvents"
    #     InputDevice     "cursor"        "SendCoreEvents"
    #     InputDevice     "eraser"        "SendCoreEvents"
    Inputdevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
Section "Module"
    Load        "glx"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by knix on 2008-09-05
> **nanog said:**
> How to fix nvidia (at least until tseliot fixes dkms bug)

```

cd /var/lib/dkms/nvidia
sudo rm -rf 177* 173*
sudo apt-get install --reinstall linux-image-2.6.27-2-generic

```
You may need to modify "2.6.27-2" with whatever kernel version you are using (e.g. 2.6.27-1 etc.).
Credits: tseliot via plun.

Note: sudo rm -rf is dangerous so be careful with this command.

i purged the drivers, removed the modules, reinstalled the kernel, and then installed the driver. works for me.

Thanks

---

### Post by Nullack on 2008-09-05
> **philinux said:**
> I went to bed. I is on London time!

Thanks Phil - that nvidia config conf wont play nice with randr and other things. Its better to let X autoconfigure as much as possible. Many old ways of doing things in the conf can break new x tech like randr.
